classified_questions.question: Can you describe what the expression \[\prod_{k = 1}^{12} \prod_{j = 1}^{10} (e^{2 \pi ji/11} - e^{2 \pi ki/13})\] represents?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the polynomial $P(x)$ when expanded, and what is its relation to the roots $e^{2 \pi ki/13}$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we relate the polynomial $x^{11} - 1 = (x - 1)(x^{10} + x^9 + x^8 + \dots + x^2 + x + 1)$ to the roots $e^{2 \pi ji/11}$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: By substituting $x = e^{2 \pi ji/11}$ into $P(x)$, what do we get?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the simplified product \[\prod_{j = 1}^{10} (e^{2 \pi ji/11} + 1)?\]
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the polynomial $Q(x)$ when expanded, and what is its relation to the roots $e^{2 \pi ji/11}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we relate the polynomial $Q(-1)$ to the product \[\prod_{j = 1}^{10} (e^{2 \pi ji/11} + 1)?\]
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Lastly, what is the value of the product \[\prod_{j = 1}^{10} (e^{2 \pi ji/11} + 1)?\]
classified_questions.label: b
